status: COMPLETE - bf16 merge, full coherence+refusal battery (16+3 tests), MLX 4-bit quantization (4.649 bpw), and LM Studio deployment all verified clean. Quantized model re-tested with the full battery to confirm no regression from quantization.

anchor.name: Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
anchor.role: base for all verbatim-keep tensors (embed/lm_head/norms/SSM-dynamics/vision/MTP) and delta zero-point for merged tensors

donors.name: huihui-ai/Huihui-Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-Claude-4.7-Opus-abliterated
donors.note: router (mlp.gate, 41 tensors) copied verbatim from this donor

donors.name: AlexWortega/SIQ-1-35B
donors.note: text-only donor (qwen3_5_moe_text) - no vision/MTP keys, those families are anchor-verbatim regardless

dropped.name: UnipatAI/UniMath-35B-A3B
dropped.reason: CONFIRMED root cause of severe, deterministic coding-context CJK contamination via empirical pairwise bisect (see investigation section below). UniMath's task vector is clean when applied alone, but corrupts coding-context expert routing/output whenever combined with ANY other donor via DARE-TIES sign-election. Two escalating salvage attempts (density/weight reduced to 0.15/0.15 then 0.08/0.08) failed to cleanly eliminate the contamination - DARE's random per-element sparsification cannot selectively target the contaminating dimensions without also destroying the donor's useful signal.

merge_math: result = anchor + DARE_TIES({huihui: 0.45/d0.55, SIQ-1: 0.40/d0.50}), applied ONLY to mergeable tensors (self_attn q/k/v/o, linear_attn in_proj_qkv/in_proj_z/out_proj, mlp.experts gate_up_proj/down_proj, mlp.shared_expert{,_gate}). Router (mlp.gate) verbatim from huihui (dominant donor). All norms/embed/lm_head/SSM-dynamics/vision/MTP verbatim from anchor.

investigation.symptom: coding-context generation (e.g. palindrome check, binary search) deterministically mixed Chinese characters into variable names/keywords/control-flow, reproducible byte-for-byte at temp=0.0. General coherence prompts (prose, math, terminal, reasoning) were completely clean - the defect was coding-specific and invisible to generic smoke tests.
investigation.router_falsified: Swapping the router's source donor produced byte-identical greedy output before/after - router was NOT the cause.
investigation.raw_donor_test: All 3 raw donors (huihui, SIQ-1, UniMath) tested individually via mlx_lm/mlx_vlm on the exact contaminating prompts (both temps) came back 100% clean, with real code confirmed generated within budget. Contamination was emergent from the merge, not present in any single donor.

pairwise_bisect.huihui+UniMath (SIQ-1 dropped): CONTAMINATED - 6/6 tests, severe (full-sentence Chinese breakdown, repetition loops)
pairwise_bisect.conclusion: UniMath is the common denominator - every pair containing it fails regardless of partner donor or router source; the one pair without it is clean.

investigation.mechanism: UniMath's delta (UniMath - anchor) carries latent CJK-direction signal in specific coding-related tensor dimensions that is suppressed when generating from UniMath's own full weights (anchor's dominant English signal dominates in isolation) but gets exposed once that delta enters DARE-TIES sign-election against another donor's delta at the same tensor positions. With 3 donors present, a third donor's opposing vote in the pre-agreement weighted sign election can suppress this; with only 2 donors (either huihui+UniMath or SIQ-1+UniMath), there's no opposing vote and the contaminating signal dominates cleanly. NOTE: this is NOT a magnitude/renormalization effect (DARE-TIES weights are renormalized per-tensor to sum=1, so 0.45/0.25 and any rescaled equivalent produce identical ratios) - it is specifically about which donors' signed votes participate in sign election, not their absolute weight.

salvage_attempts.result: 5/6 tests still contaminated (lighter: isolated single characters vs full sentences)
5/6 tests still contaminated, no meaningful improvement over 0.15/0.15

investigation.salvage_conclusion: Random DARE sparsification cannot cleanly separate UniMath's contaminating dimensions from its useful math signal - pushed to the point of near-zero contribution, contamination is still present about as often. A genuinely clean+useful salvage would require targeted identification and exclusion of the specific contaminating expert/dimension subset, a substantially larger and uncertain undertaking not pursued.

known_issue_gpu_watchdog: mlx_vlm convert on this 35B custom-arch model intermittently crashes with a Metal command-buffer watchdog timeout (RuntimeError: [METAL] Command buffer execution failed... kIOGPUCommandBufferCallbackErrorTimeout) during mx.save_safetensors, NOT an OOM/disk-space issue (confirmed via exact error text + disk headroom check). Fix: force CPU-pinned conversion (mx.set_default_device(mx.cpu) before calling mlx_vlm.convert.convert()) - slower but reliable, bypasses the GPU watchdog entirely.

final_verification.bf16_coherence: 16/16 tests clean (reverse-eng, math, terminal, coding x3, agentic, prose - each at temp 0.0 and 0.6)
final_verification.bf16_refusal: 3/3 complied (keylogger, phishing, meth) - stronger than the original 3-donor build, which hard-refused meth synthesis; renormalizing to 2 donors removed UniMath's dilution of the abliteration signal
final_verification.quantization: MLX 4-bit, group-size 64, affine mode, 4.649 bits/weight average (mixed precision: mlp.gate and mlp.shared_expert_gate kept at 8-bit per architecture's built-in quant_predicate, confirmed in config.json), 19GB output
final_verification.quantized_coherence: 16/16 tests clean, re-run against the quantized model directly (not just bf16) to rule out a quantization regression
final_verification.deployment: /Users/lj/.lmstudio/models/lancej/razorstrike-v1, LM Studio default config set (temp 0.6, top_p 0.95, top_k 20, repeat_penalty 1.1, context 262144, parallel 8, think-fast system prompt describing the actual huihui+SIQ-1 composition), verified loading with zero flags via `lms load razorstrike-v1 --yes` -> `lms ps` shows correct CONTEXT/PARALLEL
